excerpt: الحکمۃ ضالۃ المومن فأِنیّٰ وَجَدَھَا فَھُوَ أَحَقُّ بِھَا — Wisdom is the lost property of the believer; wherever he encounters it, he has the greatest right to claim it.
source_work: Traditions of Islamic jurisprudence and ethics
source_authority: High (widely attested in Islamic scholarly literature)
source_location_hint: Common in Islamic legal and ethical discourse

excerpt: وقال وهب بن منبه أوحى الله إلى نبي من أنبياء بني إسرائيل — Wahb ibn Munabbih's report of divine revelation to a prophet of the Children of Israel concerning the qualities of the Prophet Muḥammad.
source_location_hint: Attributed to Ibn Abī Ḥātim's Tafsīr

excerpt: قسمت الصلاة بيني وبين عبدي نصفين ولعبدي ما سأل — God has divided the Prayer into two halves between Himself and His servant, with the latter's requests granted.
source_authority: High (widely attested in major ḥadīth collections)
source_location_hint: Commonly cited in discussions of Sūrat al-Fātiḥah

excerpt: أنا اوصیک بخمس لِیُکَمِّلُ اللہ خصال الخیر کلہ — I counsel you with five precepts that God may perfect all excellence within you.
source_authority: High (commonly found in Islamic wisdom literature)
source_location_hint: Widely cited in Islamic exhortatory texts

excerpt: کونوا فِی الدّنیا أضیافا، و اتّخذوا المساجد بیوتا — Be sojourners in this world; make the houses of God your true homes.
source_location_hint: Common in Sufi and ethical literature

excerpt: اللهم أنت أحق من ذكر، وأحق من عبد — O God, You are most worthy of remembrance and most worthy of worship...
source_work: Supplications and devotional literature (possibly al-Sahifa al-Sajjadiyya or related sources)
source_authority: High (widely attested in Islamic devotional texts)
source_location_hint: Similar to prayers in al-Sahifa al-Sajjadiyya attributed to Imam Zayn al-ʿābidin

excerpt: قَالُوا۟ بَلَىٰ وَلَكِنَّكُمْ فَتَنتُمْ أَنفُسَكُمْ وَتَرَبَّصْتُمْ وَٱرْتَبْتُمْ وَغَرَّتْكُمُ ٱلْأَمَانِىُّ حَتَّىٰ جَآءَ أَمْرُ ٱللَّهِ وَغَرَّكُم بِٱللَّهِ ٱلْغَرُورُ — Yes, you were with us, but you led yourselves into fitna, procrastinated, doubted, and were beguiled by false hopes until God's command came.
فیہ نبأُ ما قبلکم و خبر ما بعدکم و الحکم ما بینکم — Within it [the Qur'ān] is the news of those who came before you, tidings of those who shall come after, and judgment between you.
source_author: Prophetic tradition (attributed to the Prophet Muḥammad)
source_location_hint: Common in discussions of Qur'ānic purpose and application

excerpt: أنا مولى من ضرب بسیفین و طعن برمحین وصلى القبلتین وبایع البیعتین وهاجر الهجرتین — I am the mawlā of him who struck with two swords, thrust with two spears, prayed toward both qiblah-directions...
source_work: ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib tradition and Imāmī sources
source_author: Attributed to ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib
source_authority: High (central to Shīʿa theology and Islamic literature)
source_location_hint: Widely cited in Imāmī and broader Islamic sources

excerpt: سئل قنبر مولى من أنت؟ فقال: أنا مولى من ضرب بسیفین — When Qanbar was asked whose slave he was, he replied with the full declaration of allegiance to ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib.
source_work: ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib tradition; Shīʿa and Islamic historical sources
source_author: Qanbar (freedman of ʿAlī); tradition attributed to ʿAlī
source_location_hint: Central to ʿAlī's characterization in Islamic tradition
